rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 19, 2011, line 2, Defendant Greenpoint Mortgage Funding, Inc., Bank Of America Home Loans, Gmac Mortgage, Llc'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint - Defendants Bank of America and GMACM¿s demurrer to the second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to all the causes of action alleged against the moving parties. rulings.Case Title: EDIMAR SANTOS et al VS. NEWLIN BROTHERS CONSTRUCTION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 19, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANT NEWLIN BROTHERS CONSTRUCTION, INC. MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD -Calvo Fisher & Jacob¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for defendant Newlin Brothers Construction, Inc. is granted. Good cause has been shown for the motion and it has not been contested by the defendant whose counsel seeks to withdraw. Plaintiffs¿ opposition lacks merit. If defendant does not obtain new counsel, plaintiffs can seek to strike defendant¿s answer and seek a default and default judgment. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hek

rulings.Case Title: JULIA MILLER VASQUEZ et al VS. GRASSPORTS CAMPAIGNS INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order For Preliminary Approval
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 19, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT GRASSROOTS CAMPAIGNS, INC Ntc Of Motion For Order For Preliminary Approval- Joint Motion for Preliminary Approval of Class Action Settlement and other relief is denied. Page 6 of the memorandum states that ¿courts must consider several relevant factors, including ¿the strength of the plaintiffs¿ case, the risk, expense, complexity and likely duration of further litigation, the risk of maintaining class action status through trial [and] the experience of ¿ counsel.¿ Very little, if any, information is provided on any of the aforementioned mandatory factors and, even when information is provided, it is done in a conclusory fashion with little or no evidentiary support. Moreover, other than the statement that the ¿proposed Class has over one thousand members,¿ no information is provided as to the likely size of the class. Nor is there any information about the likely payment to each member of the class. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/hek

rulings.Case Title: ST. FRANCIS BAY - ONE HUNDRED ONE CRESCENT WAY VS. TOP VISION DEVELOPMENT, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 19, 2011, line 7, DEFENDANT JAMES E. ROBERTS-OBAYASHI CORPORATION, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Line 7 St. Francis Bay v. Top Vision, 497142 -Cross-complainant James E. Roberts-Obayashi¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication against twelve cross-defendants is denied in its entirely. Cross-complainant failed to satisfy its burden of production that any of the claims for which it seeks to be defended by any of the cross-defendants is outside the exception language of the indemnity clause (¿excepting only such liability ¿ caused solely and exclusively by the fault or negligence of the Contractor¿). The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 19, 2011, Line 14, DEFENDANT GREENPOINT MORTGAGE FUNDING INC., DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Greenpoint¿s Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to toll the statute of limitations under application of the discovery rule. Plaintiff has adequately pled all causes of action. Defendant raises several factual issues not appropriately decided on a demurrer. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: PARON CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC et al VS. ROTHSTEIN, KASS & COMPANY P.C.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 19, 2011, Line 15, DEFENDANT ROTHSTEIN, KASS & COMPANY, LLP DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to Second Amended Complaint is overruled in its entirety. Niether the in pari delicto doctrine nor the economic loss rule bars plaintiffs¿ claims at this stage of the case. Plaintiffs have pled facts sufficient to allege each of the four causes of action. Defendant's Requests for Judicial Notice are denied. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: DELFIN MENDOZA et al VS. 155 TURK STREET ASSOCIATES, L.P. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 19, 2011, Line 16, DEFENDANT MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Meridian¿s Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the breach of contract and breach of implied covenant causes of action and overruled as to all other causes of action that are challenged by the demurrer.. As to the breach or contract cause of action, plaintiffs need to clarify that the leases are written and either attach the leases or plead their material terms. As to the implied covenant cause of action, plaintiffs must plead the existence of a contract. All other causes of action are sufficiently pled. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)
